examples.summary: Naval guns with a nominal three-inch bore; designs varied across anti-torpedo-boat, anti-aircraft, secondary-battery, and shore-support roles, with differing ammunition and fire-control systems.
A British dual-purpose naval gun whose role emerged amid competing 4.7-inch and 5.25-inch programmes and debates over surface and anti-aircraft armament.
A field examining how goals, resources, organizations, choices, and action combine, using historical cases and competing schools of thought to explain strategic outcomes.
The body of fictional material treated as authoritative, often arranged in tiers that distinguish on-screen works from supporting media, authorial intent, and contradictory sources.
